{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|50.72|N|133|53|6.56|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[Tōhaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest burial mound in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middle of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest burial mounds in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[grave good]]s such as [[bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middle Kofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middle Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[Tōhaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middle[[Category:middle]] of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[grave good]]s such as [[bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middle[[Category:middle]] Kofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middle[[Category:middle]] Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|27|50.72|N|133|53|6.56|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[Tōhaku District]], [[Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[grave good]]s such as [[bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)]][[en:Kitayama Kofun]][[ja:北山古墳 (湯梨浜町)]][[zh:北山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118520]] Wikidata name: Kitayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118520]] Wikidata name: Kitayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around [[:en:Lake Tōgō|Lake Tōgō]], located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from [[:en:Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|Matsuzaki Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)]]

Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|WD=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|WD=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|WD=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|WD=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|WD=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|WD=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|WD=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|WD=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|WD=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|WD=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|WD=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:11 commons]]{{template-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|WD=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District|1=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|WD=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō|1=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|WD=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station|1=Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|WD=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|WD=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District|1=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|WD=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō|1=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|WD=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station|1=Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|WD=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|qid=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District|1=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|qid=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō|1=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|qid=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station|1=Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|qid=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama)}} *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/C9CC99D529C2981D4925796F0007FCF0?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.yurihama-kankou.jp/まちの紹介/まちの歴史-伝統-文化/ Yurihama Tourist Bureau home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Kitayama Kofun | native_name =北山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kitayama Kofun (Yurihama), zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kitayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.464089|N|133.885156|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|26|27.30|N|133|49|55.95|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kitayama Kofun'''|北山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nohana Kitayama neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|qid=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama|1=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|en|Tōhaku District|qid=Q1131949|lt=Tōhaku District|1=Tōhaku District}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217219|title= 北山古墳|trans-title= Kitayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in Tottori Prefecture and is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 5th century (middleof the Kofun period).
==Overview== The area around {{ill|Lake Tōgō|en|Lake Tōgō|qid=Q3070802|lt=Lake Tōgō|1=Lake Tōgō}}, located in the central part of Tottori Prefecture, is an area where many of the largest Kofuns in the San'in region are located and was a center of Kofun period culture. Located near the tip of the Kitayama hills that extend along the southern shore of Lake Tōgō, The Kitayama Kofun is the largest keyhole-shaped tumulus in the San'in region, with a total length of 110 meters, and is orientated to the west-southwest. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. During an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1966, a pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the posterior circular portion; this chamber had a 6.2-meter-long, 4.4-meter-wide gravel bed and a drainage ditch, but had been robbed in antiquity and no artifacts were discovered. There was a second burial in a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} two meters to the south of the burial chamber. This sarcophagus measures 1.7 meters in length, 0.5 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in depth and was intact. In addition to human bones, many [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, and ''[[magatama]]'' were excavated. A third possible burial location was found three meters north of the burial chamber and a number of artifacts were recovered from this spot. On the exterior of the tumulus, many fragments of various ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' (cylindrical, figurative, "morning-glory" shaped, etc.) from the early middleKofun period were excavated from the mound, along with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and it is estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the middleKofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about five minutes by car from {{ill|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|en|Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)|qid=Q899411|lt=Matsuzaki Station|1=Matsuzaki Station (Tottori)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 110 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 12 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 62 meters long x 5.5 to 9.5meters high ;"neck" portion: 43 meters wide
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|qid=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}}
